title: rosa tomentella f. obtusifolia
intro: Rosa tomentella f. obtusifolia, also known as the wild rose, is a perennial shrub native to the mountainous regions of China, characterized by its dense, thorny stems and clusters of small, fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Chinese medicine, where it is used to treat digestive issues, fever, and inflammation. The health benefits of Rosa tomentella f. obtusifolia include its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce stress, improve sleep, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, tinctures, and infusions, as well as being incorporated into topical creams and salves to promote skin health and wound healing.
